edges.target: General Association of Korean Residents in Japan
edges.evidence_quote: The Tosei-kai was originally a reported far-right organization of anti-communist activism led by Machii as a sympathizer of Kanji Ishiwara, which was in conflict with the North Korea -associated General Association of Korean Residents in Japan.
edges.context: Tosei-kai (predecessor of Toa-kai) was in conflict with the General Association of Korean Residents in Japan.

edges.evidence_quote: The Toa-kai is a member of a bakuto fraternal federation named the Kanto Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kanto -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Matsuba-kai , and the Soai-kai .
edges.context: Toa-kai is a member of the Kanto Hatsuka-kai federation.

edges.evidence_quote: The Toa-kai is a member of a bakuto fraternal federation named the Kanto Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kanto -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Matsuba-kai , and the Soai-kai .
edges.context: Toa-kai and Sumiyoshi-kai are fellow members of the Kanto Hatsuka-kai federation.

edges.evidence_quote: The Toa-kai is a member of a bakuto fraternal federation named the Kanto Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kanto -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Matsuba-kai , and the Soai-kai .
edges.context: Toa-kai and Inagawa-kai are fellow members of the Kanto Hatsuka-kai federation.

edges.evidence_quote: The Toa-kai is a member of a bakuto fraternal federation named the Kanto Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kanto -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Matsuba-kai , and the Soai-kai .
edges.context: Toa-kai and Soai-kai are fellow members of the Kanto Hatsuka-kai federation.

edges.evidence_quote: The Toa-kai has aligned itself with the largest known Yamaguchi-gumi syndicate since the syndicate's Taoka era in the 20th century, and has been closely supported by Shinobu Tsukasa , the sixth-generation godfather of the Yamaguchi-gumi, since 2005 when the sixth era of the Yamaguchi-gumi officially started.
edges.context: Toa-kai has aligned with and been supported by Yamaguchi-gumi since the Taoka era and since 2005.
edges.time_period: since the Taoka era in the 20th century; since 2005

edges.evidence_quote: The Toa-kai's notable branch organizations include the Yoshimi-kogyo ( 誼興業 ) .
edges.context: Yoshimi-kogyo is a branch organization of Toa-kai.

edges.evidence_quote: Gibo met with Machii through Kodama, founding an affiliate of the Tosei-kai in Okinawa.
edges.context: Makoto-kai was an affiliate of Tosei-kai (predecessor of Toa-kai) in Okinawa.

edges.evidence_quote: Okinawa's underworld has been known for its exclusiveness since the 20th century, where the dominating Kyokuryu-kai has persistently attacked and violently expelled any yakuza syndicate attempting to enter the island.
edges.context: Kyokuryu-kai attacks and expels yakuza syndicates entering Okinawa, including Toa-kai.

edges.evidence_quote: The reason why the Toa-kai has been able to be active on the island may be because the group has historically been more oriented to legitimate businesses, and of its gentle stance, in contrast to other yakuza syndicates which have attempted to expand their influences into Okinawa with heavy violence, such as the Yamaguchi-gumi and especially the Dojin-kai .
edges.context: Dojin-kai attempted expansion into Okinawa with heavy violence, contrasting with Toa-kai's gentle stance.
